steps.title: Track Victor's frustration
steps.content: Victor says even learned philosophers had only partly uncovered nature and that deeper causes remained unknown. His image of fortifications blocking the "citadel of nature" shows frustration with ordinary scientific methods and with how little they can reveal.

steps.title: Rule out misreadings
steps.content: A wrongly says laypeople know more than scientists, when the passage says both know little. B says science gives clearer understanding, and C says Victor is impressed by scientific advantages. The passage instead shows him doubting whether conventional science can provide the deeper answers he seeks. The answer is D.
